contact.contactDisclaimer: We receive a significant number of emails each day. If you’ve asked for a reply, we’ll aim to respond as soon as we can, although during parliamentary recesses it may take slightly longer for us to respond. We’re usually able to reply more quickly to emails than to post. Please note, the Committee does not look at individual cases or specific complaints.

news.heading: UKRI shares first detailed comparisons of past and future spending under new ‘bucket’ model
news.teaser: Professor Sir Ian Chapman, Chief Executive of UKRI, has written to the Committee Chair, Dame Chi Onwurah, to provide previously unavailable information about its new 'bucket'-led approach to funding research.

news.heading: Angela Rayner MP and Lord Michael Gove to give evidence to Housing Committee on Draft Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill
news.teaser: The HCLG Committee questions former Secretaries of State Angela Rayner MP and Lord Gove for its inquiry on the draft Commonhold and Leasehold Reform Bill.

news.heading: Transport Committee get its inquiry into supercharging the EV transition under way with a focus on the current state of charging infrastructure
news.teaser: The Committee explores regional disparities in the current public charging infrastructure and ask whether it has the capacity to cope with a future increase in demand.

news.heading: Decisions of the Petitions Committee, 24 February 2026
news.teaser: Decisions of the Petitions Committee, 24 February 2026.
news.imageAltText: Grand Committee Room Palace of Westminster. A close up of microphones on a table with green leather upholstered seats in front of, and behind, the table

news.heading: AI and EdTech: MPs launch new inquiry examining technology’s role in education
news.teaser: MPs will examine the role of artificial intelligence (AI) and EdTech in education, in a new inquiry launched today.

news.heading: Call for evidence: Legislative scrutiny of the Courts and Tribunals Bill
news.teaser: The Justice Committee has issued a call for evidence to inform its scrutiny of the Courts and Tribunals Bill.

news.heading: Justice Committee Chair comment: Courts and Tribunals Bill
news.teaser: Chair of the Justice Committee and Labour MP Andy Slaughter said:

news.heading: Committee concerns on role of CMA Chair require risk mitigations from ministers – Pre-Appointment Report
news.teaser: Committee concerns focus on significant potential conflicts of interest and on challenge to Ministers in investment decisions that deliver market consolidation over improved consumer welfare

news.heading: MPs write to Government over cultural venue restoration funding deficit
news.teaser: The Culture, Media and Sport Committee has written to the Government after DCMS-commissioned research into the repair, maintenance and renewal of cultural venues in England highlighted a reported funding deficit of around £2 billion.

news.heading: CMS Committee presses Government on support for live comedy sector
news.teaser: The Culture, Media and Sport Committee has called for an update on the progress the Government is making on its commitments to support the live comedy sector.
